content.full_text:
Farn Por.rrrcal PnACTTcES CorraMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620. Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
November 20,2002
Paul Miller
Paul Miller for City Council
589 Anxe Hills Drive
Simi Valley,CA93065
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-290
Dear Mr. Miller:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTION
How should a controlled committee report a refund of an expenditure made for
services never rendered, when the expenditure has already been reported?
CONCLUSION
The amount and source of the returned checks should be reported as
miscellaneous increases to cash.
FACTS
on october 9,2002, you mailed two campaign checks to a slate mailer
organization - one check in the amount of $220 and the other in the amount of $640. You
reported this expenditure on a campaign statement. The two checks were not cashed, and
were retumed in the mail on October 23,2002, with the explanation that the mailer did
not intend to mail in Simi Valley.
I Government Code sections 81000 * g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. 02-290
Page No. 2
ANALYSIS
A payment is not a contribution if it is "clear from the surrounding circumstances
that it is not made for political purposes." (section 82015.) It is clear from the
circumstances that the return of your checks was not a contribution. A controlled
committee must report the amount and source of any miscellaneous receipt. (Section
84211(l).) The returned checks should be reported on form 460, schedule I, as
miscellaneous increases to cash. Because the amount exceeds $100, the report should
include the date received, the source, the description, and the amount.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By: Hal Dasinger
Political Reform Consultant
HDie
